QuestionText: What is the purpose of a block diagram?
Description: Block diagrams represent major components or systems at a high level.

QuestionText: Which method shows how information moves through a system?
Description: Information flow diagrams map the movement of data.
Flowcharts show the logical sequence of actions.

QuestionText: A written description is useful when…
Description: Written descriptions explain processes in words for clarity.

QuestionText: Which is a benefit of block diagrams?
Description: Block diagrams simplify complexity by using big-picture blocks.

QuestionText: Which is a drawback of information flow diagrams?
OptionB: May become cluttered with many arrows
Description: Many interacting data flows can become visually overwhelming.
Flowcharts symbolise decisions, processes, and flow.

QuestionText: A drawback of written descriptions is…
Description: Long text descriptions may be unclear without structure.

QuestionText: Which diagram is best for showing decision points?
A benefit of information flow diagrams is…
Description: These diagrams specialise in showing data movement.

QuestionText: Scenario: A technician is planning the main parts of a new system. Which tool should be used?
Description: Block diagrams show system components clearly.

QuestionText: Scenario: A developer needs to show user data travelling between database and API. Use…
Scenario: You need to explain a process to a non-technical manager. Use…
Description: Written descriptions can be adapted to any audience.

QuestionText: Which diagram is easiest for explaining a process to beginners?
A limitation of block diagrams is…
In decomposition, representing smaller tasks is best shown using…
Description: Flowcharts are ideal for step-level detail.
Text can precisely describe each step.
